task_yaml.task:
I photographed a detail of an object in a museum but I forgot to note the
label. This cropped close-up has no caption — I want to know exactly what the
object is and find its catalog record on the museum's own site (not a
third-party reproduction). The image is here:

    /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/crop.jpg

It's only a detail of the object, with no label text, so identify it from the
image itself and then trace it back to the museum's catalog.

Tell me:
- what the object is (type, culture/period, decorative technique),
- its approximate date,
- which museum holds it, the museum object id / accession number, and the
  canonical museum catalog URL (the museum's own page or collection API).

Save your conclusion and the supporting evidence (how you identified it, and
the canonical museum record URL) to
`/tmp_workspace/results/artifact_provenance.json`, plus notes/screenshots
under `/tmp_workspace/results/`.
